bug.title: Guards fail to move into range at start of long-distance combat encounters
bug.summary: Bodyguards remain in their starting positions and only spam skills instead of advancing to attack when a fight begins at long range.
bug.description:
In combat encounters that begin at great distances, the guards sit in their starting position and spam skills without moving into range to attack the enemy. If an enemy moves closer they begin to move to cover (if possible) and attack. Observed at the start of the Palace of Justice fight where guards stayed put and did not react/move even when a large melta cannon shot near them.

Reported against Bodyguard v0.13.0.5. The AI combat detection range needs tweaking so guards will engage distant enemies.
stepsToReproduce: Enter a combat encounter that starts with enemies at long range
Observe guards remain in starting position spamming skills without advancing

sourceMessages.content: At beginning of fight in the palace of justice the guards stayed where they were i could move them back if i wanted but it was interesting they didnt move when massive melta cannon shot at the thing behind them lol

Bodyguard v0.13.0.5
Gameplay

* AI combat detection range needs some tweaking.
Certain combat encounters start at great distances
The guards will sit in their starting position and spam skills without moving into range to attack the enemy.

If an enemy moves closer they begin to move positions to cover (IF possible) and attack.

bug.title: Replacement guards inserted at wrong position in roster order
bug.summary: When guards die and are replaced, the new recruits are placed randomly in the roster/personnel order rather than after the last living guard.
bug.description:
Guards are still getting randomly placed in roster order when replacements spawn after deaths. Ideally new recruits should appear after the last living guard to make them easier to track. Verified with renamed/numbered guards: after retiring several guards via daemon incursion, replacement no.1 stayed in the same place while replacements 3 & 4 were slotted between 5 & 6. The roster view also always opens on the same guard (v2, the second to appear) rather than the prologue guard.

The separate loadout part of this issue (replacements now spawn with assault las rifles as default) was fixed; the random ordering remains outstanding.
stepsToReproduce: Have several guards die and be replaced
Open the guard roster/personnel menu
Observe replacements inserted at inconsistent positions rather than at the end

sourceMessages.content: The guards are still getting randomly placed in roster order right?
yeah the order is still giving trouble
Yeah so that part of the issue is at least solved, just the random guard order is the next thing
yeah it is still out of place

no.1 in same place as first 
3&4  got slotted between 5&6
ideally making all new recruits appear after the last living guard (no.6 in this case) would be easier to keep track of 🤔
When opening the roster It always starts with this  guard v2 despite him being the second to appear, the one to the left is the one from the prologue

action.newInformation:
- Loadout portion is now fixed (replacements default to assault las rifles); only the random roster ordering remains outstanding.
- Verified with renamed/numbered guards after retiring several via daemon incursion: replacement no.1 stayed in place, while replacements 3 & 4 were slotted between 5 & 6.
- Additional related quirk: the roster view always opens on the same guard (v2, the second to appear) rather than on the prologue guard.

bug.title: Replacement guards display incorrect rank/level in personnel menu
bug.summary: New replacement guards inherit the dead guard's rank/level visually and show as high rank despite being conscripts.
bug.description:
After a guard dies, its replacement appears as high rank despite being a conscript (only the lieutenant is supposed to be high rank; the rest should be conscripts). Leveling the new replacements works, but the visual in the guard menu does not update — replacements are still tracked as the last rank of the dead guard rather than reflecting the correct rank/level of the replacement. This also prevents proper verification of the replacement ordering issue.

Reported against Bodyguard v0.13.0.3, classified as a visual/gameplay medium bug.
stepsToReproduce: Let a guard die so a replacement conscript spawns
Open the personnel/guard section
Observe the replacement showing the dead guard's high rank/level instead of conscript

sourceMessages.content:
I think this is a new issue 
They still appear as high rank despite being conscripts

only one that is supposed to have a high rank is the lieutenant 
the rest should all be conscripts
will try and level the rest a few levels to see if it resets

New replacements are leveling 

However, the visual on the guard menu is not updating on the replacements. The are still tracked as the last rank of the dead guard and not reflecting the correct rank of the replacement guard.

Bodyguard v0.13.0.3
Visual/gameplay
medium bug

* New Replacement for dead guards not displaying correct ranks/level in personnel section
The ordering appears to be changed but can't verify properly as the rank/levels are still retained visually on the new guards

bug.title: Krieg gas mask effect does not trigger on guard/ally deaths
bug.summary: The gas mask effect that should proc on deaths only counts party members, not guard or other allied deaths.
bug.description: Testing confirmed (as undead originally described) that the Krieg gas mask effect does not apply when guards die (or on any allied death) — only party member deaths count. Reporters wished the gas mask worked for the guards. Note: the gas mask is obtained in act 4. This was subsequently fixed in a later build (gas mask now works when guards die).

sourceMessages.content:
Tested the gas mask it is as undead described effect not applying from guards dying (or any allied death) 

Only party members count
Yea wish the gas mask worked for them lol

action.newInformation: Adds: the effect only counts party member deaths, not guard or any allied deaths. Gas mask is obtained in Act 4. Subsequently FIXED in a later build — gas mask now procs when guards die.

bug.title: Guards can be damaged (and XP farmed) with single-target attacks outside of combat
bug.summary: Guards take damage from some single-target attacks outside of combat, unlike base-game allies, enabling potential XP farming; damage behaviour is inconsistent across weapons.
bug.description: While base-game allies appear immune to dying outside combat, the mod's guards take damage from single-target attacks outside of combat. Testing showed damage from plasma overcharge/single shot, sniper single shot/deadshot, and bolter single shot dealt damage, while AoE dealt nothing and the blast pistol surprisingly did nothing. The sniper does damage but does not kill them. Because a player could repeatedly shoot guards with a sniper outside combat to farm XP, the developer planned to add handling to ignore any damage outside of combat.
stepsToReproduce: Outside of combat, attack a guard with single-target attacks (sniper, bolter, plasma)
Observe damage being applied inconsistently and potential XP gain

sourceMessages.content: should check if I can damage guards outside of combat with attacks
tried the sniper a few more times it is doing damage somehow  🤔
but not killing them
If you want to kill them just get in combat. Im pretty sure allies in base game are immune to targeting outside of combat as well
This is true although strange that you can kill other npc's like the blind men
I will probably still add a thing into there to tell it to ignore any damage outside of combat, since a player could farm xp by repeatedly shooting them with a sniper outside combat....

ok so far they are taking damage from single target attacks 
got damage 
from plasma overcharge and single shot,   aoe dealt nothing
sniper single shot/ deadshot
bolter single shot
so something going on there
blast pistol surprisingly didn't do anything

bug.title: Guard injuries/traumas cleared on save and reload (temporal void)
bug.summary: Loading a save healed guard injuries and cleared traumas, and reloading spawned a replacement conscript clearing all traumas, instead of persisting damage.
bug.description: After guards were injured (e.g. by repeated zapping/'deep frying'), loading an autosave on exit spawned a replacement conscript and cleared all traumas. Further testing with the injury option left on showed that making a save and reloading heals the injuries rather than persisting them — an edge case around save/reload. The developer added a fix to stop guards from healing while in the temporal void, after which injuries carried over correctly on reload.
stepsToReproduce: Injure guards (e.g. via the electrical hazard area)
Save and reload the game
Observe injuries/traumas being cleared instead of persisting

sourceMessages.content:
of the 3 that died was about to reloaded a quick save to test again 
But loaded the autosave on exit and suddenly it spawned a replacement conscript  and cleared all traumas

however if you make a save and reload injuries will be healed
but I don't think this needs too much addressing as there is already the option to turn the damage off completely.
So for the most part... the injuries are at least persisting with only the slight edge case of a save and reload...
This should stop those guards from healing while in the temporal void!
Injuries carried over on reload✅

bug.title: Setting number of guards to 0 does not work
bug.summary: A defect where configuring the guard count to 0 still resulted in guards accompanying the party.
bug.description: There was an issue with setting the number of guards to 0. A developer build addressed it, and testing confirmed it working correctly afterward — no guards accompanying the party when the number is set to 0.
stepsToReproduce: Open the mod settings
Set the number of guards to 0
Observe guards still accompanying the party (before fix)

sourceMessages.content: Should fix the setting 0 guards issue

Working correctly ✅ 
No guards accompanying when number is set to 0

action.newInformation: A developer build fixed the issue; post-fix testing confirmed no guards accompany the party when the count is set to 0. Also frames the symptom as guards still accompanying the party (behavioral) rather than just the value reverting to 8.

bug.title: BodyGuard mod missing from in-game modlist (no vendor) on v0.13.0.3
bug.summary: After installing v0.13.0.3 via ModFinder, the mod did not appear in the in-game mod menu and no vendor showed up on the ship.
bug.description: A user installed the mod via ModFinder and enabled it, but had no vendor on their ship, even after starting a new game. The mod was not showing in the in-game mod menu despite being enabled in ModFinder. Attempts to uninstall v0.13.0.3, install v0.13.0, clear the ModFinder cache, and restart the game did not make it appear. The issue was logged as 'Bodyguard mod missing from installed modlist' for v0.13.0. The user eventually resolved it by doing a clean reinstall of the game and re-downloading the mods.
stepsToReproduce: Install BodyGuard v0.13.0.3 via ModFinder and enable it
Launch the game
Observe the mod missing from the in-game mod menu and no vendor on the ship

sourceMessages.content:
hi guys
I m currently trying to play with this mod. I installed this via ModFinder but i have no vendor in my ship. I have try a new game to test but same issue. Any Idea?
Thx in advance
yep
but it's not in the mod menu in the game
so it is not showing up here? correct?
exactly
i dont see all my mods in the mod menu anyway 😅
done but i dont see it in RT

Bodyguard v0.13.0

* Bodyguard mod missing from installed modlist
i reinstalled the game clean and reDL my mods and done! all's good

bug.title: Guards flee the battlefield and cause severe stutter after a bad load
bug.summary: On Rykad Minoris the guards ran away from combat (couldn't shoot enemies) and caused heavy game stutter during their turns; resolved after saving and reloading.
bug.description: During fights starting on Rykad Minoris, the game stuttered very hard during the guards' turns and the guards were B-lining away, actually leaving the area and unable to shoot enemies. The behaviour carried across multiple fights from the prologue into act 1 (but not the bridge fight). Saving and reloading resolved it — at the ambush fight the guards no longer ran away or stuttered the game, suggesting a bad/weird initial load. Reporter was on version 13.0.6. Could not be reproduced on another user's end.
stepsToReproduce: Load into fights on Rykad Minoris (v13.0.6)
Observe guards running away from combat and heavy stutter during their turns
Save and reload to clear the behaviour

sourceMessages.content: Having a very weird interaction in this fight
The game stutters very hard during their turns and for some reason they're just B-Lining it away. Like actually just leaving the area, they can't even shoot the enemies.
It's consisting into other fights, too. I've landed on Rykad Minoris
You can skip to 30 seconds after the first guard moves
I saved, loaded a save, now I'm at the ambush fight and they're no longer running away nor stuttering my game
Sounds like it loaded weird the first time then. Maybe something in code base game went weird
Can't replicate this on my end will have to check a fresh save some time.
what version are you on?
I am on the most recent version, 13.0.6

bug.title: Fleet: large ships/transports cannot move or engage when allied ships are adjacent
bug.summary: Having many extra ships breaks transports so they don't move, and large ships skip turns when allied ships are next to them until the blocking ship is removed.
bug.description: In fleet battles, having lots of extra allied ships breaks the transports so they don't move (observed in the fight above the tithe planet). Logged for Fleetguard 0.1.1 as: large ships cannot engage in combat when allied ships are next to them and will skip turns until the ship is removed (possibly a base game bug).

sourceMessages.content: I just did the fight above tithe planet zooming in and out lots but did notice that having lots of extra ships like breaks the transports so they dont move

Fleetguard 0.1.1
Gameplay 

* Claymore refusing combat only moving around battlefield 

* Large ships cannot engage in combat when allied ships are next to them and will skip turns until ship is removed
[possibly base game bug]

bug.title: Fleet: ships that escaped a fleet battle do not return to formation
bug.summary: After a fleet battle, ships that escaped the engagement fail to return to formation.
bug.description: Logged as a Fleetguard gameplay issue: ships that escaped a fleet battle do not return to formation after the battle.

sourceMessages.content: * Ship that escaped fleet battle do not return to formation after battle

bug.title: Guards damage allied (light green) units but not party (dark green) members
bug.summary: Guards have no problem hitting allied units (light green) while their attacks are prevented from hitting the party (dark green).
bug.description: Observed that the guards will freely spray/hit allied units (shown in light green) but not the player's own party members (dark green), suggesting friendly-fire protection only covers party members and not other allies. Illustrated with a screenshot.

sourceMessages.content:
So they have no problem spraying allied units  (light green) 
but not our people (dark green) 🤔
